home.name: Botox & Injectables
home.description: Expression-line treatment, facial balancing and customized injectable planning.

home.name: Laser Treatments
home.description: Targeted protocols for pigmentation, acne marks, redness, pores and texture.

home.name: Hair Restoration
home.description: Medical assessment and regenerative options for thinning and shedding.

home.name: Medical Dermatology
home.description: Consultation for acne, dermatitis, infections, lesions and pathologic cases.

home.name: Skin Boosters
home.description: PDRN, exosomes and hydration-focused protocols for skin quality.

home.name: Combination Plans
home.description: Layered treatment plans built around goals, downtime and budget.

treatments.name: Hair loss treatment
treatments.description: Hair shedding and thinning can have hormonal, nutritional, inflammatory, genetic or medication-related causes. Assessment may include scalp examination, history review, laboratory requests and close monitoring before treatment is selected.
items: Medical treatment for suitable patterns of hair loss
Scalp care and regenerative procedures when appropriate
Progress photography and response-based plan adjustments
images: Medical team performing a hair-restoration procedure
Clinicians carrying out a hair transplant procedure
Hair strands in a comb illustrating hair shedding

treatments.name: Acne treatment
treatments.description: Acne care is built around the type and severity of breakouts, sensitivity, pigmentation risk and the presence of scarring. The goal is to control active disease first, then address marks and texture safely.
items: Prescription or clinic-directed topical and oral care
Comedone management, peels or device-assisted treatments
Scar and post-acne pigmentation planning after control
images: Professional facial treatment under a clinical light
Dermatologist treating a patient in a modern clinic
Skin specialist examining a patient's face

treatments.name: Pathologic skin cases
treatments.description: Rashes, infections, changing lesions and persistent symptoms need diagnosis—not cosmetic guesswork. A dermatologist reviews the history and examines the skin before recommending tests, medication, monitoring or a procedure.
items: Dermatitis, psoriasis, fungal and bacterial conditions
Moles, cysts, warts and other skin growths
Persistent itching, discoloration or unexplained changes
images: Doctor examining a patient's skin in a clinical setting
Dermatologist using a digital device during skin assessment
Dermatologist performing a clinical skin procedure

treatments.name: Laser resurfacing & more
treatments.description: Laser and light treatments can address selected concerns involving texture, scars, pores, pigment, redness and unwanted hair. Device, wavelength and settings are chosen for the diagnosis and skin type—not simply the trend.
items: Resurfacing for acne scars, fine lines and rough texture
Selected pigment and vascular concerns
Laser hair reduction and combination treatment plans
images: Clinician preparing a medical laser device
Medical professional performing a laser skin procedure
Practitioner performing a laser treatment in a clinic

treatments.name: Botox® & neuromodulators
treatments.description: Botulinum toxin treatment temporarily relaxes selected muscles. Careful facial assessment, conservative dosing and precise placement help preserve natural expression while addressing appropriate lines or medical indications.
items: Facial movement, symmetry and treatment priorities
Medical history, contraindications and aftercare
Expected onset, duration and review schedule
images: Patient receiving a facial injectable treatment
Man receiving a forehead botulinum toxin treatment
Practitioner performing a cosmetic injectable procedure

treatments.name: Skin boosters
treatments.description: Skin boosters are injectable or procedure-assisted treatments selected to support hydration, texture and overall skin quality. Products differ substantially, so formulation, authenticity and suitability should be discussed with the physician.
items: Hyaluronic-acid hydration protocols
PN/PDRN-based regenerative treatment where appropriate
Combination plans with energy-based procedures
images: Patient receiving a facial skin injectable treatment

treatments.name: Minor surgical procedures
treatments.description: Selected skin lesions can be managed through a planned office procedure after examination. The method depends on the diagnosis, location, size, scarring risk and whether tissue should be sent for laboratory analysis.
items: Biopsy or removal of suitable moles, cysts and benign growths
Wart procedures and selected nail or wound care
Local anesthesia, sterile technique and scheduled follow-up
images: Close-up of a precise clinical skin procedure
Medical team working in a sterile procedure room
Dermatology professional performing a skin treatment
